title: Hollywood Park
name: Hollywood Park
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for the limited Blanco Road/US 281 commercial edge of Hollywood Park, a small residential village north of San Antonio.

sections.html: Hollywood Park is a small, largely residential incorporated village north of San Antonio along the US 281 corridor, positioned between the city's North Side and the faster-growing Bulverde and Comfort/Boerne areas further out. Commercial property here is limited almost entirely to a narrow band along Blanco Road and the 281 frontage, which shapes a much narrower search than a full commercial submarket.

sections.heading: Commercial Stock Along the Blanco Road and 281 Frontage
sections.html:
Small retail buildings, professional offices, and a handful of service tenants line the Blanco Road corridor and the 281 frontage road, serving the surrounding residential village and the broader 281-corridor population passing through toward the Hill Country. There is minimal industrial or multifamily commercial activity within the village itself, so an exchange buyer targeting Hollywood Park is looking specifically at this small-format retail and service product.

Because the village is almost entirely residential by design, the commercial footprint has stayed narrow even as growth has accelerated in nearby Bulverde and Garden Ridge, giving existing commercial parcels here a scarcity value that larger, newer-development corridors don't have.

sections.heading: What Drives Demand in Hollywood Park
sections.html: The 281 corridor carries substantial pass-through traffic between San Antonio and the growing Hill Country suburbs, supporting the frontage-road retail and service tenants even though the village's own residential population is small. Continued growth in Bulverde, Garden Ridge, and the broader north corridor adds commuter traffic that benefits Hollywood Park's limited commercial strip, and the surrounding household income levels support rents that outperform the village's small size would otherwise suggest.

sections.heading: Closing Coordination for a Hollywood Park Property
sections.html:
Given the village's small, largely residential zoning framework, confirming what commercial use is actually permitted on a specific parcel is a necessary step before a property is placed on a written identification. Coordination for a Hollywood Park file typically covers:

  • Village-specific zoning and permitted-use confirmation for the parcel
  • Written identification meeting the unambiguous-description standard under Treasury Regulation 1.1031(k)-1
  • Lease abstract and tenant credit review for the specific property
  • Lender preflight against Hollywood Park's thin comparable sales data
  • Exchange agreement and assignment documents routed to the qualified intermediary before funding

This tracks documentation and timeline; it is not a substitute for the investor's own legal or tax review.

sections.heading: Watchouts for Hollywood Park Investors
sections.html: The narrow commercial footprint means very limited inventory turns over at any given time, so an investor relying on a single Hollywood Park identification without a backup risks having no fallback if that specific deal falls through inside the 45-day window. Thin comparable sales data can also complicate lender appraisal, so pulling comps from the broader 281 corridor, rather than the village alone, is usually the more workable approach.

faqs.q: What kind of commercial property exists in Hollywood Park?
faqs.a: Small retail buildings, professional offices, and service tenants along the Blanco Road corridor and the US 281 frontage road. There is minimal industrial or multifamily commercial activity within the village's residential footprint.

faqs.q: Why is Hollywood Park's commercial inventory so limited?
faqs.a: The village is designed and zoned almost entirely for residential use, so the commercial footprint has stayed narrow even as growth has accelerated in nearby Bulverde and Garden Ridge along the same 281 corridor.

faqs.q: Does pass-through traffic on 281 help Hollywood Park retail?
faqs.a: Yes, substantial commuter traffic between San Antonio and the growing Hill Country suburbs supports the frontage-road retail and service tenants even though the village's own resident population is small.

faqs.q: Should I identify a backup property given how thin Hollywood Park inventory is?
faqs.a: Most investors do, since the narrow commercial footprint means very limited inventory turns over at any given time, leaving little room to adjust if a specific identified property falls through inside the 45-day window.

faqs.q: How should I approach appraisal comps for a Hollywood Park property?
faqs.a: Pulling comparable sales from the broader US 281 corridor, rather than the village alone, is usually more workable given how few properties trade within Hollywood Park's own small commercial footprint.
